title: ferray-linalg: dot/matmul/outer all flatten via iter().copied().collect() before computing
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Throughout products/mod.rs, every function starts with 'let a_data: Vec<T> = a.iter().copied().collect()' and 'let b_data: Vec<T> = b.iter().copied().collect()' to flatten the array into a Vec, then does manual indexing into the flat data. This: (1) allocates O(n) temporary memory, (2) destroys any SIMD-friendly layout, (3) ignores that the data may already be contiguous (as_slice()). At minimum, check as_slice() first and only collect if needed. Better: use faer's matmul for 2D cases (see #413) and work with stride-aware indexing for ND cases.
